description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with stages lint, build, test, build deb, build docker, publish, docs, downstreams, and release. In the build stage, the matrix "build" job (JAVA_VERSION 11‑17) runs on openjdk:${JAVA_VERSION}-jdk, executes `export VERSION=$(./version.sh)`, `echo "Version: ${VERSION}"`, and `./gradlew installDist`; the jobs "build java11 aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu" and "build java11 x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu" extend the .build template with JAVA_VERSION=11 and SIGNALD_TARGET set. The "build deb aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu" and "build deb x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu" jobs use custom builder images, export VERSION, modify debian/control architecture via `sed`, run `gbp dch …` and `dpkg-buildpackage -b`, then move the resulting signald_* files to the job artifacts. The "clang-format" lint job uses debian:buster-backports, installs clang‑format‑11 and runs `tools/format.sh`. Docker build jobs "container amd64" and "container arm64" extend .docker-buildx, log in to the dependency proxy and GitLab registry with podman, set VERSION and DOCKER_PLATFORM, build non‑root and root images with `podman build … -t "${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:${VERSION}-${DOCKER_PLATFORM}(-non-root)"`, and push them; the "container manifest" job pulls both images, creates multi‑arch manifests with `tools/podman-multiarch-manifest.sh`, tags and pushes to Docker Hub, and cleans up temporary tags via Docker Hub API. Publishing jobs "publish debian packages" and "publish stable debian packages" run `tools/aptly-publish.sh` with DISTRIBUTION unstable or stable respectively. The "release" job runs on tags, installs python3‑requests and executes `python3 ./tools/release/ci-create.py`. The "signald.org-branch" docs job installs Hugo and Go, clones the website repo, generates protocol docs, builds the site with Hugo, and stores the `public` directory as an artifact. Downstream job "signald.org" triggers the signald.org pipeline on the main branch. Unit test jobs "unit tests aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu" and "unit tests x86" install JDKs, run `./gradlew test`, and publish JUnit reports. All jobs include appropriate rules (protected‑branch checks, manual allow_failure, only tags/main) and use caches/artifacts as defined.
